title: Rolling Stones Shake Up the Band
story: The Rolling Stones rocked the music world in 1969. On June 8, guitarist Brian Jones was asked to leave the famous band. It was a big change for the group that had been making hit songs together. The Stones didn't miss a beat, though. They quickly found a new guitarist named Mick Taylor to join their lineup.
activity_question: Who replaced Brian Jones in the Rolling Stones?
follow_up_question: Can you name another famous rock band from the 1960s?

title: The Stones Rock with Jumpin' Jack Flash
story: Guitar riffs blasted through radios as the Rolling Stones unleashed their electrifying new single. On June 8, 1968, 'Jumpin' Jack Flash' hit the airwaves, marking a return to the band's bluesy rock roots. Fans couldn't help but dance to its catchy beat and Mick Jagger's powerful vocals. The song quickly climbed the charts, becoming one of the Stones' most beloved hits.
activity_question: What type of music did 'Jumpin' Jack Flash' represent for the Rolling Stones?
bonus_fact: The song's title was inspired by Keith Richards' gardener, Jack Dyer.

title: Cricket Star's Debut: Tony Greig Shines
story: Excitement filled the air at Old Trafford cricket ground on June 8, 1972. Tony Greig, a tall and talented player, stepped onto the field for his Test Cricket debut. Facing the tough Australian team, Greig showed his skills and determination. This match marked the beginning of a remarkable career for the future England captain.
activity_question: Where did Tony Greig make his Test Cricket debut?
follow_up_question: What country was Tony Greig playing against in his debut match?

title: Jazz Legend's Unexpected Absence
story: Jazz piano virtuoso Oscar Peterson faced a tough setback in 2007. A special all-star concert at Carnegie Hall was planned to honor his incredible musical career. But on June 8, Peterson had to cancel his appearance due to sudden illness. Despite his absence, the show went on, celebrating the maestro's lasting impact on jazz.
activity_question: Where was the all-star performance honoring Oscar Peterson supposed to take place?
follow_up_question: What instrument was Oscar Peterson famous for playing?

title: Laughs End for Smothers Brothers Show
story: Giggles and gags came to a close as 'The Smothers Brothers Comedy Hour' aired its final episode on CBS-TV in 1969. This popular variety show had kept viewers in stitches with its mix of music, comedy, and sometimes controversial satire. Tom and Dick Smothers, the brotherly duo at the heart of the program, brought their unique blend of humor to living rooms across America every June 8 and throughout the year.
activity_question: What type of show was 'The Smothers Brothers Comedy Hour'?
bonus_fact: The Smothers Brothers often used comedy to comment on social issues of their time.

title: A Comedy Star Is Born
story: Laughter filled the air as Keenan Ivory Wayans entered the world in New York City. Born on June 8, 1958, he would grow up to become a famous comedian. Wayans later created the hilarious TV show In Living Color, which launched the careers of many funny performers and changed the face of comedy on television.
activity_question: What famous TV show did Keenan Ivory Wayans create?
follow_up_question: Can you name any other famous comedians?

title: A Star is Born: Robert Preston
story: Lights, camera, action! Robert Preston burst onto the scene in 1918, born on June 8 in Newton, Massachusetts. This future star would dazzle audiences on stage and screen. Preston's talent shone brightest in musicals like The Music Man and Mame. He even blasted off into sci-fi adventure in The Last Starfighter!
activity_question: In which musical did Robert Preston famously perform?
bonus_fact: Robert Preston's full name was Robert Preston Meservey.
